content:
3 min read
Just now

--

Even a flower doesn’t begin with itself.G E N one of the most famous abbreviations floating around the world at present. Generative... or I would say generation.
What actually is a generation?
The time where we are witnessing floods of generated ideas and content. What actually is generation ?
Generation of an idea? Generation of novelty?
Is it just something that no one has heard of, no one has thought of?
There are numerous definitions of it that float around. What I had moved with is the definition that "novelty" means "distinction," distinction from what exists, and distinction from what anyone thinks.
Yeah, I’m getting a bit extreme, but stay with me; I’ll cool it down by the end.
So, I was just going through some software development tool, and it had some dependencies. Searching through them, I dug into operating systems, kernels, how they work with hardware, and fundamentally, how they’re all executed by transistors at the very base.
It was fascinating.
And it gave me a deep respect towards evolution , how, not only in this particular field but in any field, any tool, any device, literally even something as simple as a sewing needle to machines as advanced as satellites, what we witness today is not the result of one year of development, or one generation, or even one decade.
These are the results of thousands of years, millions or even billions of human lives swept from the world, and trillions of hours being spent.
Yes.
This is the result of incremental innovation.
There’s a beautiful quote by Isaac Newton:
“If I have seen further, it is by standing on the shoulders of giants.”
It beautifully describes all of this.
Centuries ago, the development of algebra and geometry resulted in the development of calculus. It didn’t appear out of thin air.
Modern large language models didn’t emerge out of thin air either. Someone sitting in the 1940s wondered, “Could machines think?” And by incrementally building on that question, we reached a stage where machines are not only imitating humans but are even surpassing us in some domains.
So what do we see?
It is never about novelty being completely different from what already exists.
Innovation is never about bringing things out of thin air.
It’s always about adding that 1%.
Just 1%.
One improvement.
One connection nobody noticed.
One better question.
One abstraction.
One simplification.
One insight.
Yes, it’s really that simple.
Just thinking:
How can I add my 1% to the world I came into and to the world I leave?
If each person is making that 1% change, the result compounds into what we witness today.
A simple metal turned into a wire became responsible for electricity reaching our homes.
Yeah, that’s the level of compounding we are witnessing.
The realization I went through, and what I would highlight, is that it’s actually more about assembling.
We often disregard any abstraction because we know it’s made from these building blocks.
But maybe not all abstractions are bad.
Maybe most abstractions are actually the required new ideas.
Maybe a new formation and assembly of existing ideas is actually innovation.
Maybe true novelty is orchestrating and threading existing ideas together.
Yeah…
Because i would say,
You just can’t solve life in one life.
Not because it cannot be solved, but because no single lifetime is enough to begin from nothing. Every generation continues a conversation started long before it.
The newness, the originality, depends on how you interpret those existing ideas and how you increment them with your own understanding.
The conclusion probably is this:
Curiosity, the search for first principles, the search for fundamentals, and the scrutiny of everything are definitely necessary.
But always remember that you need to build on top of what’s already there.
Because,
that’s how civilization progresses.
It’s about accepting what exists, understanding it deeply, and then building over it.
Because you don’t know what percentage of those trillions of human hours you have actually got.
And that’s precisely why i said
Don’t be original.
Because the search for originality often stops us from creating.
…Haaah…
Thats it…
for today,
for more intriguing thoughts you can follow me (btw, here also think, do you really need to follow me?? 🤔😆)
Signing off…
“People popularized their thoughts, I will popularize the ability to have a thought.”
-nishchay
Bye… 👋😊
